title: Chandraghanta Chalisa
intro: Shri Chandraghanta Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Goddess Chandraghanta
description:
|| Shri Chandraghanta Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Chandraghanta maa kalyani pavan hai shubh naam.
Gaura girija roop hai teesra sun lo gyaan.

|| Chaupai ||
Chandraghanta mata kalyani.
Mamta-mai mata vardani.
Girija ka hi roop kahave.
Maiya bhakton ko ati bhave.
Sahas sab mein devi bharti.
Sant janon ki raksha karti.
Devon ko vipada se bachaya.
Hathon mein tha shastra uthaya.
Vaahan singh ki kare savari.
Raudra roop ki mahima bhari.
Kar mein chakra sudarshan dhari.
Paapi ran mein sakal pachhade.
Durga ka bhi roop kahave.
Bhakton ki raksha ko aave.
Ran mein bharti maa hunkara.
Bal jag mein hai jiska nyara.
Treta mein jab sankat chhaya.
Raudra roop tab tumne dikhaya.
Indra sinhasan asuran cheena.
Sor bahut sab heene keenha.
Mahishasur daanav ek bhari.
Devon ki sena thi pachhadi.
Devon ne tab dhyan lagaya.
Shiv ne shakti punj banaya.
Gaura ne tab roop tha dhara.
Durga ka keenha vistara.
Kar mein tab ghanti thi suhai.
Chandra ghanta maa tab kahlayi.
Ghanta ran mein maat bajaya.
Asuran ko tab maar giraya.
Devi ne hunkar bhari thi.
Sahas ki lalkar bhari thi.
Mahishasur ka mardan keenha.
Maiya ne sankat har leenha.
Dharam dhwaja jag mein lehrave.
Devta saar the harshave.
Chandraghanta devi thi kahave.
Durga banke shakti dikhave.
Ran mein saare asur pachhade.
Astra shastra se daanav mare.
Granth kahe aisi bhi kahani.
Santo muniyon ne hai jaani.
Shiv jo himalaya nagari aaye.
Bhoot pret aur gan bhi laye.
Ati bhayankar roop vishala.
Koi mota aur koi kala.
Dekh ke murchhit ho gayi maina.
Bhay se vyakul rote naina.
Devi ko dhang samajh na aaya.
Gaura dhari thi tab kaya.
Gaura ne tab shakti dikhayi.
Chandra ghanta tab bani mahamayi.
Ghanta bhari tab tha bajaya.
Shiv ke ganon ko dara bhagaya.
Shiv ne jab tha roop nihara.
Jana shakti sakal pasara.
Tab devi chandraghanta kahave.
Vaar inka suhave.
Kar mein ghanti shobhit inki.
Roop pe sab hai mohit jinki.
Singh savari maat ko pyari.
Shobhit vrishabh ki bhi hai savari.
Teesre din jo vrat ko dhare.
Maiya saare kaaj sanvare.
Devi Durga maat kahave.
Abhay mudra mein badi suhave.
Jan-jan chola lal chadhave.
Maiya se aashish bhi pave.
Phal phoolon se sajta dwara.
Lagta dwara sabse nyara.
Chandra ghanta devi kalyani.
Sukh se bharti maa vardani.
Maiya mangal karne wali.
Sukh se jholi bharne wali.
Karti sabki purn aasha.
Man mein bharti hai vishwasa.
Chalisa jo inka gaata.
Vo jan to bhav se tar jaata.
Devi sabke dukhde harti.
Sukh se sabki jholiyan bharti.

|| Doha ||
Chandra ghanta chalisa ka itna hi hai saar.
Prem se jo padta jan hai maiya kar bhav se paar.
meaning:
|| Shri Chandraghanta Chalisa (with English Meaning) ||

|| Doha ||
Chandraghanta maa kalyani pavan hai shubh naam.
(Mother Chandraghanta is the benefactor, and her name is pure and auspicious.)
Gaura girija roop hai teesra sun lo gyaan.
(This is the third form of Gaura and Girija; listen to this wisdom.)

|| Chaupai ||
Chandraghanta mata kalyani. Mamta-mai mata vardani.
(Mother Chandraghanta is the benefactor, a compassionate mother and giver of boons.)
Girija ka hi roop kahave. Maiya bhakton ko ati bhave.
(She is known as the form of Girija (Parvati) and is very dear to her devotees.)

Sahas sab mein devi bharti. Sant janon ki raksha karti.
(The Goddess fills everyone with courage and protects the saints.)
Devon ko vipada se bachaya. Hathon mein tha shastra uthaya.
(She saved the Gods from calamity and raised weapons in her hands.)

Vaahan singh ki kare savari. Raudra roop ki mahima bhari.
(She rides a lion and the glory of her fierce form is immense.)
Kar mein chakra sudarshan dhari. Paapi ran mein sakal pachhade.
(Holding the Sudarshan Chakra in her hand, she defeats all sinners in the battlefield.)

Durga ka bhi roop kahave. Bhakton ki raksha ko aave.
(She is also called the form of Durga, who comes to protect her devotees.)
Ran mein bharti maa hunkara. Bal jag mein hai jiska nyara.
(Mother roars in the battlefield; her strength in the world is unique.)

Treta mein jab sankat chhaya. Raudra roop tab tumne dikhaya.
(When crisis loomed in the Treta Yuga, you showed your fierce form.)
Indra sinhasan asuran cheena. Sor bahut sab heene keenha.
(Demons snatched Indra's throne, causing great chaos and despair.)

Mahishasur daanav ek bhari. Devon ki sena thi pachhadi.
(Mahishasura was a great demon who defeated the army of the Gods.)
Devon ne tab dhyan lagaya. Shiv ne shakti punj banaya.
(The Gods then meditated, and Shiva created a cluster of power.)

Gaura ne tab roop tha dhara. Durga ka keenha vistara.
(Gaura then took that form, expanding the manifestation of Durga.)
Kar mein tab ghanti thi suhai. Chandra ghanta maa tab kahlayi.
(There was a beautiful bell in her hand, and thus she was called Mother Chandraghanta.)

Ghanta ran mein maat bajaya. Asuran ko tab maar giraya.
(Mother rang the bell in the battlefield and struck down the demons.)
Devi ne hunkar bhari thi. Sahas ki lalkar bhari thi.
(The Goddess gave a roar, filling the battlefield with a challenge of courage.)

Mahishasur ka mardan keenha. Maiya ne sankat har leenha.
(She crushed Mahishasura and took away the troubles of the Gods.)
Dharam dhwaja jag mein lehrave. Devta saar the harshave.
(The flag of Dharma waves in the world, and all Gods rejoice.)

Chandraghanta devi thi kahave. Durga banke shakti dikhave.
(She is called Goddess Chandraghanta, showing her power as Durga.)
Ran mein saare asur pachhade. Astra shastra se daanav mare.
(She defeated all demons in the battlefield and killed them with her weapons.)

Granth kahe aisi bhi kahani. Santo muniyon ne hai jaani.
(Scriptures and saints tell a story like this.)
Shiv jo himalaya nagari aaye. Bhoot pret aur gan bhi laye.
(When Shiva came to the city of Himalayas, he brought ghosts, spirits, and his Ganas.)

Ati bhayankar roop vishala. Koi mota aur koi kala.
(They had extremely fierce and huge forms; some were fat, some were black.)
Dekh ke murchhit ho gayi maina. Bhay se vyakul rote naina.
(Seeing them, Maina (the mother of Parvati) fainted, her eyes weeping in fear.)

Devi ko dhang samajh na aaya. Gaura dhari thi tab kaya.
(The Goddess did not find a solution, so she changed her form as Gaura.)
Gaura ne tab shakti dikhayi. Chandra ghanta tab bani mahamayi.
(Gaura then showed her power and became the Great Mother Chandraghanta.)

Ghanta bhari tab tha bajaya. Shiv ke ganon ko dara bhagaya.
(She rang the heavy bell and scared away Shiva's Ganas.)
Shiv ne jab tha roop nihara. Jana shakti sakal pasara.
(When Shiva saw this form, he realized the extent of her power.)

Tab devi chandraghanta kahave. Vaar inka suhave.
(Then she was called Goddess Chandraghanta; her day is auspicious.)
Kar mein ghanti shobhit inki. Roop pe sab hai mohit jinki.
(The bell in her hand is beautiful, and everyone is enchanted by her form.)

Singh savari maat ko pyari. Shobhit vrishabh ki bhi hai savari.
(Mother loves the lion ride, and the ride of the bull also adorns her.)
Teesre din jo vrat ko dhare. Maiya saare kaaj sanvare.
(He who fasts on the third day, Mother fixes all his tasks.)

Devi Durga maat kahave. Abhay mudra mein badi suhave.
(She is called Mother Durga, beautiful in the Abhay Mudra (gesture of fearlessness).)
Jan-jan chola lal chadhave. Maiya se aashish bhi pave.
(People offer red robes to her and receive her blessings.)

Phal phoolon se sajta dwara. Lagta dwara sabse nyara.
(Her entrance is decorated with fruits and flowers, looking unique.)
Chandra ghanta devi kalyani. Sukh se bharti maa vardani.
(Goddess Chandraghanta is the benefactor, filling life with happiness, the giver of boons.)

Maiya mangal karne wali. Sukh se jholi bharne wali.
(Mother is the bringer of auspiciousness, filling our lives with happiness.)
Karti sabki purn aasha. Man mein bharti hai vishwasa.
(She fulfills everyone's hopes and fills the mind with faith.)

Chalisa jo inka gaata. Vo jan to bhav se tar jaata.
(Whoever sings this Chalisa crosses the ocean of worldly existence.)
Devi sabke dukhde harti. Sukh se sabki jholiyan bharti.
(The Goddess removes everyone's sorrows and fills their lives with joy.)

|| Doha ||
Chandra ghanta chalisa ka itna hi hai saar.
(This is the essence of the Chandraghanta Chalisa.)
Prem se jo padta jan hai maiya kar bhav se paar.
(Whoever reads it with love, Mother ferries them across the worldly ocean.)

faq.a1: Her name is derived from the half-moon (crescent) on her forehead, which is shaped like a bell (Ghanta). Hence, she is called 'Chandraghanta.'
faq.q2: What is the main benefit of worshiping her?
faq.a2: Maa Chandraghanta is the goddess of bravery and fearlessness. Worshiping her removes all kinds of fear from the devotee's mind and helps in developing courage and mental strength.
faq.a3: Maa Chandraghanta governs the 'Manipura Chakra.' Her meditation helps in balancing the energy of this center.
faq.q4: When is the auspicious day to worship her?
faq.a4: The third day of Navratri (Tritiya) is dedicated to Maa Chandraghanta.
faq.q5: What is the appropriate offering (Bhog) for her?
faq.a5: Offering milk-based sweets or Kheer (rice pudding) to the Goddess is considered very auspicious.
